ops.title: getaway v2 Phase 9–10: fix wave resolutions + ship
ops.body:
# getaway v2 — review fix wave resolved and shipped (2026-07-14)

Follows [[getaway v2 Phase 9 review: findings + accept/reject]] (note 4ca252d4). Every confirmed finding fixed, adversarially re-verified, full CI green locally, shipped as 8 scoped jj commits to `main` at `19efa38efbc145a9ad34e42b4116d874e11729d0` (CI/cc-notes/Guides all green).

## Confirmed findings — all fixed
- **WALKER-1/2** (plan-trip.js): ABS_PATH hardened with `\s` exclusion; kind→subcommand allowlist (KIND_COMMANDS/STEP_COMMANDS) with slug bound to the walker's own validated slug. 4 node tests added.
- **RETRIEVAL** (shortlist.py `_cohort_select`): dropped `int(c["soft"])` from the sort — cohort order is mileage-only; soft-avoid rides ranking, never retrieval.
- **QUOTA** (store.py `reserve_quota` + seats.py): bootstrap serialized (admit only when `in_flight == 0`) — verified race-free with an 8-process test (exactly 1 admits); explicit `HTTP_TIMEOUT = httpx.Timeout(30.0)` guarantees the request ≪ 5-min reservation-TTL invariant.
- **EMPTINESS** (journeys.py expand assembly): `search_states` is now a by-leg map `{"outbound", "return"}`; a failed/quota-stopped one-way outbound sweep surfaces its `not_run{quota_budget}`/`failed{retryability}` state instead of an empty "no availability" board. finalize passes it through unchanged.
- **CUTOVER** (afford.py, seats.py op_ref): both prefs reads route through the new `prefs.load_or_empty()` seam.
- **TIEBREAK** (factors.py `_tiebreak`, from rank-finder pass): reused `_cost_vector` → `(tuple(sorted(vector.items())), journey["id"])`; no cross-program mileage sum (was a `_verdict_score`-style composite in the tie-break).
- **ASSESS VALIDATOR** (trips.py, from rank-finder 2nd pass): assess.json was the ONLY agent-written artifact with no write-boundary validator. Added `_validate_assess_artifact` constraining `factor` to judgment-kind ids (derived at runtime from `registry.factors()`), verdict enum, and exact structure — closes the deterministic/judged double-count and satisfies A2/C3.

## Two regressions caught DURING the wave (orchestrator review)
- `prefs.show()`/`_load()` RAISE `StateConflictError` on a missing prefs file → would break the README-guaranteed neutral-profile path (afford) and the env-var-only key path (seats op_ref). Fix: new public `prefs.load_or_empty()` (tolerates absence → `{}`, rejects a pre-v2 `credits` shape loudly). Both consumers route through it.
- `prefs.show()["op_ref"]` subscript → KeyError when `op_ref` is absent; `.get("op_ref")` via the seam is correct (justified by the `{}` neutral-profile case, not defensive).

## Rejected / non-findings (for the record)
- **CUTOVER trips.py:413 `show()`** — dropped (T1). No trip-doc legacy loader/marker exists; the only legacy loader (prefs `_reject_legacy`, keyed on `credits`/`travel_instruments`) would reject every trip; `show()` returning raw JSON is write-boundary-validated by design.
- **Lead A** (multi-currency cash sum) — dead code today; see [[getaway journey cash axis is single-component today]].
- **Lead B zero-mileage misroute** — unreachable (`_mileage_components` in fit.py skips cash legs; a 0-mileage award is a data impossibility); no defensive guard, per doctrine.

## Ship: 8 scoped commits (bottom→top on main)
engine(A1–A8) → registry(B1–B3) → quota(C1) → stays(B4) → walker(C2–C3) → block(B5) → gather(B3) → docs(C4). Journey-engine and composition/rank are one commit (journeys.py/trips.py/factors.py inseparable at file level). Full gate: 533 pytest, ruff, ty, 35 node, jq manifests. Live dispatch (Verification step 4) deferred — needs a user-seeded rooms.aero Touch-ID session.
